text.title: CodeQL + Copilot Autofix Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules pinning the CodeQL SAST gate, Autofix policy, and skip path.

rule.statement: `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` MUST trigger on both `push` (to default branch) and `pull_request`. Scheduled runs are SHOULD, not MUST.
rule.rationale: PRs surface new alerts before merge; push covers main-branch baseline drift.

rule.statement: Branch protection MUST make `codeql-analyze` a required status check for merge.
rule.rationale: Without required check, CodeQL alerts can be ignored at merge.

rule.statement: Copilot Autofix MUST post patches as PR suggestions only; auto-merge of Autofix patches is forbidden. A human accepts, edits, or rejects each.
rule.rationale: AI-generated patches can introduce regressions; human-in-the-loop is non-negotiable.

rule.statement: Workflow MUST declare a `language` matrix (e.g., `[javascript, python, go]`). Polyglot repos MUST list every CodeQL-supported language present in the repo.
rule.rationale: Matrix per language is the standard idiom; missing a language silently skips it.

rule.statement: For repos with Bash / Dockerfile / Terraform / PHP, AI-powered detections MUST be enabled in Code Scanning so the gate is uniform across the stack.
rule.rationale: CodeQL covers ~10 languages; the AI lane fills the gap on remaining surfaces.

rule.statement: Mass-renaming refactors (≥1k LOC churn) MUST schedule a one-shot baseline reset of CodeQL alerts BEFORE the PR opens; otherwise the PR floods reviewers with false-positive alerts.
rule.rationale: Mass refactor floods are an operational hazard; baseline reset is the documented escape hatch.

rule.statement: If repo is not hosted on GitHub OR is documentation/asset-only OR lacks GHAS license while being private, STOP.
rule.rationale: Skip applies when CodeQL has no surface or no license.
